question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: NodeMap] Can an unprivileged external user submit a /construction/payloads request with a node_account_id that is a valid string but resolves to a hiero.AccountID with a non-zero checksum that does not match any NodeMap entry, and if the checksum is ignored during comparison, bypass the node allowlist?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: Db.Pool] Can an unprivileged external user exhaust all Pool.MaxOpenConnections by submitting many concurrent /construction/submit requests, causing the transaction submission handler to block indefinitely waiting for a database connection and creating a denial-of-service that prevents all transaction submissions?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: NodeMap] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a node_account_id that is a valid NodeMap key but the corresponding hiero.AccountID has been updated in the NodeMap during a refresh, causing the transaction to be submitted to the old node while the server believes it went to the new one?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: Feature.SubNetworkIdentifier] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a SubNetworkIdentifier that is present in the request but ignored by the server when Feature.SubNetworkIdentifier is false, causing the transaction to be submitted to the main network when the client intended it for a sub-network?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: NodeMap] Can an unprivileged external user submit a /construction/submit request with a signed transaction whose protobuf body has been re-encoded with different field ordering, causing the transaction hash computed by TransactionGetTransactionHash to differ from the hash the client expects, breaking transaction tracking?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: Config.Network] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the case-insensitive network name normalization (strings.ToLower) to submit transactions with a network identifier like 'MAINNET' or 'MainNet' that bypasses case-sensitive network validation checks elsewhere in the pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: NodeMap] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a node_account_id that is a valid NodeMap entry but for a node that is currently in maintenance mode, causing the transaction to be submitted but never processed, while the Rosetta server returns a success hash?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: Http.ReadTimeout] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ReadTimeout to submit a /construction/submit request that is read just before the timeout, causing the server to process a truncated transaction body that passes deserialization but has missing or zeroed fields, resulting in a malformed transaction being submitted to the network?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: NodeMap] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a node_account_id that is a valid NodeMap entry but the AccountID's Shard or Realm does not match CommonConfig.Shard or CommonConfig.Realm, causing a cross-shard/realm transaction submission that the network rejects after fees are deducted?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: Db.GetDsn()] Can an unprivileged external user cause transaction replay by exploiting the unencrypted database connection in GetDsn() to intercept and replay transaction lookup responses, causing the Rosetta server to believe a transaction has already been processed when it has not, blocking legitimate re-submissions?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: NodeMap] Can an unprivileged external user submit a /construction/payloads request with a node_account_id that is a valid NodeMap entry, then modify the transaction's valid_start_nanos in the signed bytes before /construction/submit, causing the transaction ID to change and the submitted transaction to have a different identity than what was signed?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: Cache.MaxSize] Can an unprivileged external user set MaxSize to 0 or a negative value through a configuration injection, disabling the entity cache entirely and causing every transaction submission to perform uncached database lookups that can be exploited for TOCTOU attacks on account state?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: NodeMap] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a node_account_id that contains a valid AccountID but with an alias field set, and if the NodeMap key comparison uses the string representation that differs between alias and numeric forms, bypass the node allowlist check?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: Config.ShutdownTimeout] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a graceful shutdown (e.g., via a signal if accessible) and then submit a transaction during the ShutdownTimeout window, causing the transaction to be accepted and forwarded to the network but the response to be dropped, leaving the client without a transaction hash?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: NodeMap] Can an unprivileged external user submit a /construction/submit request with a signed transaction that has the node_account_id field set to a valid NodeMap entry but with additional unknown protobuf fields appended, causing the SDK to process the transaction with unexpected extra data that alters its behavior on the network?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: Response.MaxTransactionsInBlock] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the MaxTransactionsInBlock limit to cause a block to appear to contain fewer transactions than it actually does, enabling the attacker to submit a transaction that is executed on-chain but invisible to Rosetta-based monitoring tools?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: NodeMap] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a node_account_id that is a valid NodeMap entry but the node has been removed from the Hedera network, causing the transaction to be submitted to a decommissioned node that may process it under old consensus rules?
